artifact_scope.note: This inventory describes the current Android code path plus Firebase Authentication and the RC3 default-off Crashlytics dependency. Synthetic preview/canary content that is never participant data is not treated as collected user data. Crashlytics participant/production collection remains disabled and requires separate privacy/data-use authorization before activation.

sdk_inventory.active_use: Phone authentication only when all protected pilot bindings are configured.
automatic_processing: IP address for security and abuse prevention
Firebase user agent/device metadata and SDK/platform version information
usage_dependent_processing: Play Integrity or anti-abuse tokens used by the phone-auth flow when applicable
sdk_inventory.sharing_assessment: service-provider-processing-not-declared-as-third-party-sharing-under-current-model; final Play form must be rechecked against the exact SDK/version and contracts

sdk_inventory.active_use: SDK is release-capable but automatic collection is explicitly disabled in the main manifest. RC3 enables a default Firebase app and Crashlytics collection only in the source-controlled synthetic-canary build on an isolated CI emulator. Participant and production telemetry are disabled.
automatic_processing_when_enabled: Relevant application state at the time of an event
Firebase installation ID and Firebase user-agent data through transitive Firebase Installations
Firebase Sessions metadata used for Crashlytics quality metrics
direkt_rc3_developer_defined_data: Fixed canary kind: fatal or nonfatal
explicitly_not_attached_by_rc3: Names, phone numbers or email addresses
sdk_inventory.sharing_assessment: service-provider-processing-not-declared-as-third-party-sharing-under-current-model; participant/production collection is disabled and final Play form must be revalidated before any broader Crashlytics activation

play_data_types.collection_condition: Only when protected Firebase phone authentication is enabled and the invited user starts verification.
play_data_types.required_or_optional: required for the current approved phone-auth sign-in method
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Firebase/provider and DIREKT account lifecycle controls apply. Final deletion fulfillment, legal-hold rules and public deletion request path remain release blockers pending qualified review and implementation evidence.

play_data_types.play_category: Device or other IDs / User IDs
play_data_types.collection_condition: Firebase creates an authentication user ID; DIREKT exchanges it into server-authoritative identity/session identifiers and stores only a protected digest of the external subject server-side.
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Session identifiers expire/revoke; account identity retention/deletion follows the approved rights and legal schedule, which is not yet final for real production data.

play_data_types.play_category: Device or other IDs / Device metadata and network identifiers
play_data_types.collection_condition: Firebase Authentication automatically processes IP address, user-agent/platform metadata and Firebase app metadata for service operation, security and abuse prevention. RC3 Crashlytics adds no participant/production collection because its automatic collection remains disabled outside the isolated synthetic canary.
play_data_types.required_or_optional: automatic when Firebase Authentication is used; Crashlytics participant collection remains disabled
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Provider-controlled service retention applies; exact final form mapping must be revalidated using Firebase Android data-disclosure guidance for the exact shipped SDK versions and any future Crashlytics participant activation.

play_data_types.play_category: App activity / Consent and authentication events
play_data_types.collection_condition: DIREKT records the approved notice version, affirmative acceptance and bounded authentication/session security metadata during successful admission.
play_data_types.required_or_optional: required for controlled admission and accountability
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Bounded consent/audit history is retained according to the approved policy/legal schedule; final real-data schedule remains subject to qualified review.

not_collected_by_current_android_release: broad photos or media library access
analytics events through an analytics SDK

participant/production crash, non-fatal or ANR telemetry through Sentry or Crashlytics; RC3 Crashlytics collection is default-off and limited to an isolated synthetic CI canary until separately authorized
real provider evidence bytes through an Android upload flow
real enquiry/review/complaint text through the current synthetic preview interaction surfaces

future_or_blocked_product_data.data: provider profile, evidence, location, enquiries, contact handoff, reviews, complaints, subscription/payment records
future_or_blocked_product_data.status: backend contracts exist but the current Android production-capable release path must not declare these as active collection until the exact release candidate actually transmits them and the approved privacy/legal basis is live

future_or_blocked_product_data.data: participant Crashlytics crash/non-fatal/ANR diagnostics and associated Firebase installation/session/device metadata
future_or_blocked_product_data.status: SDK source integration exists but participant/production collection remains disabled; activation requires separate privacy/data-use approval, exact release revalidation and updated Play Data Safety declarations before shipping

security_answers_candidate.request_account_deletion_blocker: Google Play requires an in-app path and public web request route when account creation is supported. DIREKT has sign-out/session revocation but the final account deletion request/fulfillment workflow and public route are not yet production-approved.
security_answers_candidate.notes: Do not claim optional Play security badges or deletion support until evidence exists. Do not declare participant/production Crashlytics collection until a separately approved data-use decision and exact release evidence exist.

submission_boundary: The Play developer is responsible for the final declaration. Revalidate this inventory against the exact release candidate, merged manifest, SDK versions, backend behavior, approved privacy policy, Crashlytics collection mode and live account-deletion flow before submitting any closed/open/production release.
